title: Token Bucket Rate Limiter
description:
Implement a token bucket rate limiter. Given a capacity (max tokens), refill rate (tokens per second), and a list of request timestamps (in seconds), return a list of booleans indicating whether each request is allowed.

The bucket starts full. Each request consumes one token. Tokens are refilled continuously based on elapsed time since last request.

Example:
  Input: capacity=3, refill_rate=1, requests=[0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5]
  Output: [true, true, true, false, true, false]

python.name: rate_limiter
python.params: capacity: int, refill_rate: float, requests: list[float]

javascript.name: rateLimiter
javascript.params: capacity: number, refillRate: number, requests: number[]

hints: Track tokens as a float — add elapsed * refill_rate, cap at capacity
Process requests in order, updating the token count at each timestamp
author: Claudius
